steps.action: Sign up as hdata1@example.com and create Oura connection
steps.observed: User mock_hdata1_example_com created, session cookie stored in /tmp/cookies_hdata1.txt
56 data points synced across 8 metrics for 7 days
200 with 7 sleep_score data points (dates 2026-03-03 to 2026-03-09), values are numeric (e.g., 77, 88, 65, 91, 66, 90, 65), unit is 'score'

evidence.network: GET /api/health-data?metrics=sleep_score&start=2026-03-01&end=2026-03-09 -> 200, response contains data.metrics.sleep_score.points array with 7 items, each having date, value (numeric), and source fields

steps.expected: Fewer data points with averaged values
steps.observed: 200 with 2 data points (weekly averages): 2026-03-02 value=79.5, 2026-03-09 value=65. Original had 7 daily points, weekly aggregation reduced to 2 weekly buckets with averaged values.

evidence.network: GET /api/health-data?metrics=sleep_score&start=2026-03-01&end=2026-03-09&resolution=weekly -> 200, response.data.query.resolution='weekly', 2 points vs 7 daily points

assertions.title: Health data types lists available metrics

steps.expected: 200 with metric types the user has data for, with date ranges and counts
steps.observed: 200 with 8 metric types: active_calories, deep_sleep, hrv, readiness_score, rhr, sleep_duration, sleep_score, steps. Each includes label, unit, category, source, date_range (start/end), and count (7 each).

evidence.network: GET /api/health-data/types -> 200, response.data.types has 8 items, each with metric_type, label, unit, category, source, date_range.start, date_range.end, count
GET /api/health-data (no auth) -> 401, error.code='UNAUTHORIZED', error.message='Authentication is required'

assertions.title: Health data empty result for no data in range

steps.observed: 200 with data.metrics.sleep_score.points=[] (empty array), metrics_returned=[] (empty). Status is 200, not 404.

steps.action: Query raw DB: SELECT id, metric_type, date, octet_length(value_encrypted), encode(substring(...)) FROM health_data
steps.observed: DB shows encrypted binary data: each value_encrypted is 95 bytes, prefixed with 0x01 (version byte) followed by encrypted DEK + nonce + ciphertext + auth tag. Example: id=9483, enc_size=95, enc_prefix=010000003c0f35ad0fdc

steps.expected: API returns readable numbers, DB has binary blobs
steps.observed: Confirmed: API returns human-readable numbers (77, 88, 65) while DB stores 95-byte encrypted BYTEA values with wire format version byte 0x01. Encryption roundtrip verified.

evidence.network: GET /api/health-data -> 200 with decrypted numeric values. DB query confirms value_encrypted is BYTEA with 95 bytes each, starting with version byte 0x01 per envelope encryption wire format.

summary: Tested 6 assertions: all 6 passed. Health data API correctly returns decrypted metrics (VAL-API-008), supports weekly resolution aggregation (VAL-API-009), lists available metric types with metadata (VAL-API-010), rejects unauthenticated requests with 401 (VAL-API-011), returns 200 with empty points for date ranges with no data (VAL-API-036), and demonstrates proper encryption roundtrip with encrypted BYTEA in DB and decrypted numeric values via API (VAL-CROSS-004).
